tweets.text:
Majority of people here today will never remember these days.

But many largely agree these were probably the best of times.

The ultimate shop for us to gamble, the location that provided so much opportunity, and loss.

And, never forget March 2020 for pulling the plug and keeping $BTC from $0.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Dear BitMEX Users,

Today, we share with a very heavy heart that BitMEX exchange will shut down its operations, effective 23 September 2026 at 04:00:00 UTC.

The owner and operator of BitMEX, HDR Global Trading Limited, has made the difficult decision to close operations https://t.co/oWuqlh547f
tweets.full_text:
Majority of people here today will never remember these days.

But many largely agree these were probably the best of times.

The ultimate shop for us to gamble, the location that provided so much opportunity, and loss.

tweets.text:
Many folks don't realize that Vanguard is a mutual which makes it particularly hard to fathom how the current leadership justifies paying 4%/yr fees to Blackstone in a complex illiquid structure.

https://t.co/vYqKegmSTW
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Bogle is rolling over in his grave today with Wellington, Vanguard & Blackstone announcing the cutely named WVB All Markets Fund.

Class A pays 1.39%/yr for a portfolio holding 2/3 allocated to what typically costs 7bps and 1/3 in private assets at a fee load of 4.03%. Gross.
tweets.full_text:
Many folks don't realize that Vanguard is a mutual which makes it particularly hard to fathom how the current leadership justifies paying 4%/yr fees to Blackstone in a complex illiquid structure.

tweets.text:
The team shipped a bunch of new AI features/tools this week:

- Making it easier for users to test out agentic trading, with new agentic trading views in Coinbase for Agents
- A new @coinbasedev x402 SDK for agentic commerce builders
- Businesses can accept agentic payments now with @CoinbaseBiz

Our core AI thesis: the agentic economy is going to be huge, it needs new financial rails, and we’re building them.
tweets.full_text:
The team shipped a bunch of new AI features/tools this week:

- Making it easier for users to test out agentic trading, with new agentic trading views in Coinbase for Agents
- A new @coinbasedev x402 SDK for agentic commerce builders
- Businesses can accept agentic payments now with @CoinbaseBiz

Our core AI thesis: the agentic economy is going to be huge, it needs new financial rails, and we’re building them.

tweets.text:
I have a tremendous amount of fond memories of BitMEX. 

It was where I first made my money. My first liquidation — my first margin call. 

I remember trading on BitMEX during the March Covid crash, frantically setting limit orders, panicking when the exchange finally froze. 

I remember looking at the orderbook seeing about zero dollars left on the bid side, and immediately trying to send in more money, because if the industry was going down, I was going down with it.

Thank you for all the memories. May you rest in peace.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Dear BitMEX Users,

Today, we share with a very heavy heart that BitMEX exchange will shut down its operations, effective 23 September 2026 at 04:00:00 UTC.

The owner and operator of BitMEX, HDR Global Trading Limited, has made the difficult decision to close operations https://t.co/oWuqlh547f
tweets.full_text:
I have a tremendous amount of fond memories of BitMEX. 

It was where I first made my money. My first liquidation — my first margin call. 

I remember trading on BitMEX during the March Covid crash, frantically setting limit orders, panicking when the exchange finally froze. 

I remember looking at the orderbook seeing about zero dollars left on the bid side, and immediately trying to send in more money, because if the industry was going down, I was going down with it.

Thank you for all the memories. May you rest in peace.

tweets.text:
Just learnt that Hermes might just be amazing at Oil Trading after all
​
4 Wins with ~63% ROI (albeit with a small bankroll)
​
Ran an experiment that lets Hermes auto-trade on Oil Daily Up Down market
​
Hermes started with $200 bankroll, leverages Synthdata daily forecast oil signals, and executes & settle via BlockRun x402
​
If Synth agrees with the market (positive delta) by ≥ 2 percentage point / disagrees with the market (negative delta) by ≤ 8 percentage point, Hermes executes the trade
​
Might just be luck during US-Iran escalation period but excited to see how Hermes performs over the next few months
​
(BlockRun comes in handy because it helps get over Polymarket Geoblock that users might face with CLOB API)
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Man... AI is cheap but it's so expensive
​
Cost to get started (inference per 1M token) went from $18.40 to $6.07 (3x cheaper in a year)
​
But the cost to continuously run it (agentic workflows) is 23.4x more expensive
​
On top of this, more users are adopting AI, and each user https://t.co/G0COcA9f2K
tweets.full_text:
Just learnt that Hermes might just be amazing at Oil Trading after all
​
4 Wins with ~63% ROI (albeit with a small bankroll)
​
Ran an experiment that lets Hermes auto-trade on Oil Daily Up Down market
​
Hermes started with $200 bankroll, leverages Synthdata daily forecast oil signals, and executes & settle via BlockRun x402
​
If Synth agrees with the market (positive delta) by ≥ 2 percentage point / disagrees with the market (negative delta) by ≤ 8 percentage point, Hermes executes the trade
​
Might just be luck during US-Iran escalation period but excited to see how Hermes performs over the next few months
​
(BlockRun comes in handy because it helps get over Polymarket Geoblock that users might face with CLOB API)

tweets.text:
yikes. google down -7% this morning despite beating earnings because they've fallen behind in AI

people don't care about revenue they're making today they're pricing in the revenue they make once models are good enough to meaningfully accelerate different business units - search, gsuite, android etc

remember google is being priced as a vertically-integrated ai company but they've fumbled the most important layer: models.

q3 is going to be where they turn things around (im hoping) with gemini 4, but they need to show up.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
it’s official - Google’s is now cash flow negative (spending more money than they make) for the 1st time in 22 years (since they went public):

they’re spending $200B+ this year on ai. 60% on infra, 40% on data centers

this is their last hail mary - they have to put out a https://t.co/KFZOZcNqVh

tweets.text:
crazy - kimi k3 exploited one of the largest infrastructures on the internet for $50-120 and it only took 27 mins using 32 agents:

- this would have taken a team of human experts weeks and $10Ks to do. kimi did it in a single prompt < 1 hr.

- the server it exploited basically lives in your computers RAM so an attacker could run their own malicious code.

mythos-grade cyber-attacks are already here and they're coming in the form of new chinese open source

thread_tweets.text: to explain the hail mary comment: building intelligence is all that matters over the next few years. if google doesn’t have a competitive model this means *every part of their business* (search, cloud, apps, android etc) will improve at a slower rate vs others. models are like powerups, when RSI’s here they’re going update many sectors overnight (just look at what’s happening in mathematics this week). so either you’re the landlord or the renter. they could also host open models but that’s an entirely other conversation.
